Table 2: Results on the general perception and reasoning benchmark MME-RealWorld-Lite.

Figure 6: Analysis of inference behavior across rounds. Left: average number of tool calls per round. Right: average chainof-thought length per round. Both quantities decrease steadily as rounds progress, indicating that TTSP effectively exploits accumulated structured knowledge to reduce redundant visual inspection and reasoning.
Figure 3: Scalability analysis of TTSP along three dimensions: (a) model size, (b) perception width, and (c) perception depth.

Figure 2: Overview of TTSP. In each round, TTSP samples a mixture of fresh traces and guided traces for parallel perceptual exploration, filters them using entropy-based reliability scores, and distills the surviving traces into structured knowledge consisting of Confirmed Knowledge and Open Conflicts. This knowledge is injected into the next round to guide further evidence acquisition. After the final round, TTSP produces the answer via reliability-weighted aggregation over all retained traces.
